pr.title: Match 8 functions across stage, fighter, and menu TUs
pr.author: davidfeira

summary: PR #2464 matched several functions across fighter, stage, and game-mode translation units, plus one header signature refinement for gm_8016A9E8. The PR body claims 8 matched functions with ninja main.dol linking and per-function objdiff-cli 100% results; the bot report lists 12 new matches including functions and data-section items, plus 9 unmatched-item improvements. The PR also records that an earlier gricemt const-section reorder was dropped after CI surfaced a 4-byte neighboring regression.

key_files.role: Matched or completed ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90 by selecting motion state from cmd_vars and Kirby hat kind, using ftKb_Init globals for Fighter_ChangeMotionState speed/frame fields, and preserving hitlag callbacks.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90 99.82% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Adjusted Ice Climbers Special-S/Hi logic and matched ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Anim, and ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll per bot report.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: checkNanaInRange, ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll hunks
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists three ftPp_SpecialS function matches to 100.00%

key_files.role: Matched ftCo_800D0EC8 by correcting argument order to ftCo_CalcYScaledKnockback.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists ftCo_800D0EC8 99.96% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Added body for ftCo_8009CB40 as an unmatched-item improvement and matched ftCo_8009DB50 dynamics installer; changed assert file argument to __FILE__ and used bone_id local for dynamic bone slots.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists ftCo_8009DB50 to 100.00%, ftCo_8009CB40 0.00% to 92.02%, and .sdata/.sdata2 changes

key_files.role: Implemented gm_8016A9E8 as a 27-entry two-array MRU push using -2 sentinel search and head insertion.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists gm_8016A9E8 0.00% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Refined gm_8016A9E8 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to int gm_8016A9E8(u8 arg0, s8 arg1).
Added Big Blue route StageCallbacks/data definitions, adjusted OSReport data usage, matched grBigBlueRoute_8020B9D4, and improved grBigBlueRoute_8020C530 and .data/.sdata per bot report.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grBb_Route_803E5E78, grBb_Route_803E617C, grBigBlueRoute_8020B9D4, grBigBlueRoute_8020C530 hunks
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists grBigBlueRoute_8020B9D4 to 100.00%, .data improvement, .sdata match, and grBigBlueRoute_8020C530 improvement

key_files.role: Matched grPura_80211FD8 by passing the Ground_GObj through grPura_80213030 and updating its local prototype/signature.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grPura_80211FD8 and grPura_80213030 signature hunks
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists grPura_80211FD8 94.44% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Added Brinstar callback/data definitions and matched grZebes_801D8558 per PR body and bot report.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grZe_803E1A10, grZe_callbacks, grZe_803E1B2C, grZebes_801D8558 surrounding hunk
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists grZebes_801D8558 94.88% to 100.00% and .data improvement

what_changed: Matched or completed claimed functions across ftPp_SpecialS, ftdynamics, ftchangeparam, grzebes, grbigblueroute, grpura, gm_1601, and ftKb_SpecialNYs.
Refined gm_8016A9E8 declaration in gm_1601.h to int gm_8016A9E8(u8 arg0, s8 arg1), matching the recovered body.
Added or exposed stage data/callback tables in grbigblueroute.c and grzebes.c as part of matching stage setup routines.
Used PAD_STACK in ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90, ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Anim, and ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll according to the diff.
Dropped an earlier gricemt const-section reorder after CI reported a 4-byte regression in neighboring grIceMt_801F7A2C, according to the PR notes.
smart_moves: The PR body records narrow verification: ninja main.dol linked cleanly and per-function objdiff-cli diff reported 100.0% for the claimed 8 functions.
The gm_1601.h prototype was updated alongside the recovered gm_8016A9E8 body instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
The author removed the gricemt commit after CI detected a neighboring regression from const-section reorder, keeping the submitted PR focused on non-regressing matches.
The ftCo_800D0EC8 fix demonstrates a small high-leverage match: only the first and third ftCo_CalcYScaledKnockback arguments changed.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function body proves a signature, update the owning header prototype in the same PR rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1601.c implements int gm_8016A9E8(u8 arg0, s8 arg1)
raw_diff: gm_1601.h changes UNK_RET gm_8016A9E8(UNK_PARAMS) to int gm_8016A9E8(u8 arg0, s8 arg1)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Data or const-section reorder work can regress neighboring functions; if a reorder creates CI/report noise, split or drop it and resubmit with a non-regressing approach.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Notes section about gricemt commit 1e672b30a, const-section reorder, 4-byte grIceMt_801F7A2C regression, and dropping the commit

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small argument-order corrections can close near-matched helper wrappers; ftCo_800D0EC8 reached 100% by changing ftCo_CalcYScaledKnockback argument order.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800D0EC8 swaps Fighter_804D6524->x30 and p_ftCommonData->x310 positions

naming_conventions.convention: Recovered address-style symbols were generally kept as address-style names, such as gm_8016A9E8, ftCo_800D0EC8, grZebes_801D8558, grBigBlueRoute_8020B9D4, and grPura_80211FD8.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: function names listed by TU
raw_diff: implementations and edits use address-style names

naming_conventions.convention: Local role names in new code include short names such as fp, gp, ptr, bone_id, found, idx, and nana_pos when the role is apparent.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90 uses fp and msid
raw_diff: gm_8016A9E8 uses ptr and found

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was introduced narrowly in several fighter functions to satisfy stack/codegen shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Anim and ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Anim add PAD_STACK(32)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The diff shows tactic use and the PR body claims objdiff 100% for 8 functions, but no inline reviewer rationale is present for each PAD_STACK instance.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Preserved explicit user_data casts in ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90 instead of reusing fp for all reads, apparently for matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90 changes fp->cmd_vars and fp->fv.kb.hat.kind reads to ((Fighter*) gobj->user_data)->...
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No review comment explains whether this is preferred style or only a local matching shape.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Stage setup matching included materializing callback/data tables and using data-backed OSReport format/file strings in grbigblueroute.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grBb_Route_803E5E78 table and grBb_Route_803E617C struct added
raw_diff: OSReport changed from literal __FILE__ form to grBb_Route_803E617C.fmt and grBb_Route_803E61D4
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This touched .data/.sdata according to the bot report; evidence supports improvement, but broad data-ownership conclusions need curator review.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Natural loops were used in newly recovered gm_8016A9E8 and grBigBlueRoute_8020C530 instead of unrolled repeated blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_8016A9E8 uses for loops over 27 entries and shift loop
raw_diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020C530 uses for loops over 30 entries
text_corpus issue_comment: gm_8016A9E8 reached 100.00%; grBigBlueRoute_8020C530 improved to 98.72%

review_feedback.feedback: Bot report for GALE01 showed matched code improved by +0.07% / +2604 bytes and matched data by +48 bytes, with 12 new matches and 9 improvements in unmatched items.

An earlier gricemt commit with three extra matches and const-section reorder was dropped because CI surfaced a 4-byte regression in grIceMt_801F7A2C; author planned a follow-up PR with a non-regressing approach.

human_pr_text: full PR body with summary, function descriptions, verification, and notes

notes: There are no human review comments in the loaded slice.

The PR body says 8 matched functions, while the bot report lists 12 new matches including data-section items and additional function matches in ftPp_SpecialS; this record preserves both without resolving intent beyond evidence.
Verification claims are from the PR body and bot report, not independently rerun during intake.
Several data additions in stage files appear in the diff and bot report, but source ownership and split correctness are not independently established here.
PAD_STACK and explicit user_data access are treated as local matching tactics, not accepted global style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes should follow proven recovered bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2464 recovered gm_8016A9E8 as int gm_8016A9E8(u8 arg0, s8 arg1) and updated gm_1601.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in the same PR. This is a concrete example of keeping owning headers truthful after a match proves a signature.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Drop data reorder work when it regresses neighbors
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2464 notes that an earlier gricemt commit with three extra matches and const-section reorder was removed after CI showed a 4-byte regression in neighboring grIceMt_801F7A2C, with follow-up deferred for a non-regressing approach.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2464 as an example under text-before-data-matching or data-sections-and-tu-splits
source_update_candidates.text: Possible example: PR #2464 dropped an earlier gricemt const-section reorder because CI found a 4-byte regression in grIceMt_801F7A2C, even though it bundled three additional matches. This supports keeping data/const reorder work separate or non-regressing when the submitted PR is primarily code matching.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already cover this principle; curator may want to add PR #2464 as a concrete historical example rather than a new rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR #2464 as an example for truthful header updates after body recovery
source_update_candidates.text: Possible example: gm_8016A9E8's recovered body proved int return and u8/s8 parameters, and gm_1601.h was updated from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in the same PR.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source-backed example of an existing accepted standard, not a new path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: Track possible follow-up PR for dropped gricemt matches
source_update_candidates.text: The PR body says three gricemt matches fn_801F9338, fn_801F9448, and fn_801F9558 were dropped because a const-section reorder regressed grIceMt_801F7A2C, and that they would be resubmitted later with a non-regressing approach.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful for curator search/backlinking; not evidence that the follow-up exists or landed.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the explicit ((Fighter*) gobj->user_data) reads in ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90 as preferred style; evidence only shows a local matching shape.
Do not promote PAD_STACK use as a general recommendation; the PR evidence only supports narrow use in touched fighter functions with claimed matching.

Do not infer final ownership of grbigblueroute or grzebes data tables solely from this intake; the diff and bot report show data-section changes, but broader split/ownership validation is outside the slice.

Do not treat the bot report's 12 new matches as contradicting the PR title/body's 8 claimed functions; the bot includes data-section items and additional function matches not emphasized by the author.
